name: St Matthew's Church, Brixton
why: A Grade II* listed Greek-Revival church on Brixton Hill; free to look around — check opening times on the official listing.

name: Brockwell Park
why: A free 50-hectare park with a walled garden and city views; open daily — confirm access on the official source.

name: Geraldine Mary Harmsworth Park
why: A free public park around the former Bethlem site, open daily; check access on the official source.

name: Crossbones Garden
why: A free memorial garden on a historic Southwark burial ground; open on set days — confirm hours on the official source.

name: Wilton's Music Hall
why: The world's oldest surviving grand music hall; Grade II* listed and free to view — check opening times on the official listing.

name: Tower Hamlets Cemetery Park
why: A free woodland cemetery and local nature reserve; open daily — confirm access on the official source.

name: New River Walk
why: A free linear waterside walk following the historic New River; open access — confirm on the official source.

name: St James's Church, Piccadilly
why: A Grade I listed Wren church with a free-to-enter courtyard; check opening times on the official listing.

name: Paddington Street Gardens
why: A free former burial-ground garden in Marylebone; open daily — confirm access on the official source.
